name: 4-Channel Relay Controller with Web Interface
description: Web-based 4-channel relay controller for home automation and appliance control
author: ATHENA Team

metadata.content:
# 4-Channel Relay Controller with Web Interface

## Overview
This template implements a web-based 4-channel relay controller that allows you to control electrical appliances and lights through a web browser. Perfect for home automation and IoT applications.

## Features
- Web-based control interface
- 4 independently controllable relays
- Custom relay names for easy identification
- Real-time status monitoring
- Auto-off timer for safety
- Persistent state storage (EEPROM)
- Responsive web interface
- JSON API for integration
- Mobile-friendly design

## Web Interface
- **Dashboard**: Shows all 4 relays with ON/OFF controls
- **Real-time Status**: Auto-updates every 5 seconds
- **Custom Names**: Each relay has a customizable name
- **Visual Feedback**: Color-coded status indicators
- **Mobile Responsive**: Works on smartphones and tablets

## API Endpoints
- `GET /` - Main web interface
- `POST /relay` - Control individual relays
- `GET /status` - Get current status
- `POST /config` - Update configuration
- `POST /reset` - Reset all relays to OFF

## Relay Control
Send POST request to `/relay` with parameters:
```
relay=0&state=on
relay=1&state=off
```

## Configuration Options
- **Relay Pins**: Digital pins for each relay (default: 26,27,32,33)
- **WiFi Settings**: SSID and password for network connection
- **Device Name**: Custom name for the controller
- **Relay Names**: Custom names for each relay
- **Auto-Off Delay**: Automatic turn-off timer in minutes (0=disabled)
- **Scheduler**: Time-based scheduling (future feature)

## Wiring
- ESP32 5V → Relay Module VCC
- ESP32 GND → Relay Module GND
- ESP32 Digital Pin {{.relay1Pin}} → Relay Module IN1
- ESP32 Digital Pin {{.relay2Pin}} → Relay Module IN2
- ESP32 Digital Pin {{.relay3Pin}} → Relay Module IN3
- ESP32 Digital Pin {{.relay4Pin}} → Relay Module IN4
- Relay COM → Power Supply Positive
- Relay NO → Appliance Positive
- Appliance Negative → Power Supply Negative

## Safety Features
- Auto-off timer prevents accidental left-on
- EEPROM persistence remembers last state
- Web interface requires manual confirmation
- Visual status indicators
- Error handling and validation

## Use Cases
- Home lighting control
- Fan and appliance management
- Garden lighting automation
- Industrial equipment control
- Smart home integration
- Remote control systems

## Integration Options
- MQTT publishing for IoT platforms
- Voice assistant integration
- Mobile app control
- Automation rules and schedules
- Sensor-based automation

## Dependencies
- WiFi library (built-in)
- WebServer library (built-in)
- ArduinoJson v6.21.3
- EEPROM library (built-in)

## Troubleshooting
- **Cannot connect to WiFi**: Check SSID and password
- **Relays not responding**: Verify wiring and power supply
- **Web interface not loading**: Check IP address and network
- **State not persistent**: Check EEPROM functionality
- **Auto-off not working**: Verify delay settings

## Advanced Features
- Time-based scheduling
- Sensor integration
- Energy monitoring
- Scene management
- Voice control
- Mobile notifications

## Security Notes
- Change default WiFi credentials
- Use WPA2/WPA3 security
- Consider VPN for remote access
- Regular firmware updates
- Network isolation for critical systems
